title: Dal Dottore
gcseTheme: Theme 2: Local, National, International and Global Areas
scenario: You feel sick on holiday in Italy and visit a doctor.

lines.speaker: dottore
lines.english: Good morning. How do you feel?

lines.speaker: you
lines.italian: Non mi sento bene. Mi fa male la testa.
lines.english: I don't feel well. My head hurts.

lines.speaker: dottore
lines.italian: Da quando ha mal di testa?
lines.english: Since when have you had a headache?

lines.speaker: you
lines.italian: Da ieri mattina. Ho anche la febbre.
lines.english: Since yesterday morning. I also have a fever.

lines.speaker: dottore
lines.italian: Ha la tosse o il raffreddore?
lines.english: Do you have a cough or a cold?

lines.speaker: you
lines.italian: Sì, ho la tosse e il naso chiuso.
lines.english: Yes, I have a cough and a blocked nose.

lines.speaker: you
lines.italian: Un po'. Non ho mangiato molto.
lines.english: A bit. I haven't eaten much.

lines.speaker: dottore
lines.italian: No, è solo l'influenza. Deve riposare.
lines.english: No, it's just the flu. You need to rest.

lines.speaker: you
lines.english: Do I need to take medicine?

lines.speaker: dottore
lines.italian: Sì. Ecco la ricetta. Vada in farmacia.
lines.english: Yes. Here is the prescription. Go to the pharmacy.

lines.speaker: dottore
lines.english: In the square, next to the bank.

lines.speaker: you
lines.italian: Prenda le medicine due volte al giorno.
lines.english: Take the medicine twice a day.

lines.speaker: dottore
lines.italian: Beva molta acqua e dorma bene.
lines.english: Drink a lot of water and sleep well.

lines.speaker: you
lines.english: Thank you, doctor. How much do I have to pay?

practicePrompts.prompt: How would you say your arm hurts?
How would you say you have a fever?
How would you ask where the pharmacy is?
